creator: jcwells@u.washington.edu
text:
# This is a shell archive.  Save it in a file, remove anything before
# this line, and then unpack it by entering "sh file".  Note, it may
# create directories; files and directories will be owned by you and
# have default permissions.
#
# This archive contains:
#
#	linux-netscape45-communicator
#	linux-netscape45-communicator/scripts
#	linux-netscape45-communicator/scripts/configure
#	linux-netscape45-communicator/pkg
#	linux-netscape45-communicator/pkg/COMMENT
#	linux-netscape45-communicator/pkg/DESCR
#	linux-netscape45-communicator/pkg/PLIST
#	linux-netscape45-communicator/files
#	linux-netscape45-communicator/files/mailcap
#	linux-netscape45-communicator/files/md5
#	linux-netscape45-communicator/files/netscape.sh
#	linux-netscape45-communicator/patches
#	linux-netscape45-communicator/patches/patch-aa
#	linux-netscape45-communicator/Makefile
#
echo c - linux-netscape45-communicator
mkdir -p linux-netscape45-communicator > /dev/null 2>&1
echo c - linux-netscape45-communicator/scripts
mkdir -p linux-netscape45-communicator/scripts > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - linux-netscape45-communicator/scripts/configure
sed 's/^X//' >linux-netscape45-communicator/scripts/configure << 'END-of-linux-netscape45-communicator/scripts/configure'
X#!/bin/sh
X
Xsed -e "s;@X11BASE@;${X11BASE};g" -e "s;@PREFIX@;${PREFIX};g" \
X	${FILESDIR}/netscape.sh >${WRKSRC}/netscape
Xchmod +x ${WRKSRC}/netscape
X
END-of-linux-netscape45-communicator/scripts/configure
echo c - linux-netscape45-communicator/pkg
mkdir -p linux-netscape45-communicator/pkg > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - linux-netscape45-communicator/pkg/COMMENT
sed 's/^X//' >linux-netscape45-communicator/pkg/COMMENT << 'END-of-linux-netscape45-communicator/pkg/COMMENT'
XLinux Netscape Communicator suite, version 4.5
END-of-linux-netscape45-communicator/pkg/COMMENT
echo x - linux-netscape45-communicator/pkg/DESCR
sed 's/^X//' >linux-netscape45-communicator/pkg/DESCR << 'END-of-linux-netscape45-communicator/pkg/DESCR'
XNew features in 4.5 since 4.0x:
X
XSmart Browsing 
X3-Pane Integrated Mail / News Interface 
XPinpoint Addressing and Scalable Address Book 
XGreatly improved IMAP mail performance, network efficiency & interoperability. 
XPalm Pilot address book, calendar and mail synchronization 
XNew Calendar with simplified user interface
XMail and address import from text, Outlook Express and Eudora 
XSmartUpdate uninstall 
XNetscape Quality Feedback System for bug reporting 
X
XSee "/usr/local/lib/netscape/LICENSE" for the licensing terms.
X
XNote: If Java applets fail to display.  Type this as root:
X		cd /usr/X11R6/lib/X11/fonts/misc
X		/usr/X11R6/bin/mkfontdir
X		chmod 444 fonts.dir
X      And then exit and restart your X server.
X
XPeter Hawkins <thepish@FreeBSD.org>
X
XThanks to: ache@freebsd.org (for original port)
END-of-linux-netscape45-communicator/pkg/DESCR
echo x - linux-netscape45-communicator/pkg/PLIST
sed 's/^X//' >linux-netscape45-communicator/pkg/PLIST << 'END-of-linux-netscape45-communicator/pkg/PLIST'
X@exec ln -sf %f %B/communicator-4
X@exec ln -sf communicator-4 %B/netscape
X@unexec rm -f %B/netscape
X@unexec rm -f %B/communicator-4

echo c - linux-netscape45-communicator/files
mkdir -p linux-netscape45-communicator/files > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - linux-netscape45-communicator/files/mailcap
sed 's/^X//' >linux-netscape45-communicator/files/mailcap << 'END-of-linux-netscape45-communicator/files/mailcap'
X# Example .mailcap file for FreeBSD machines
X#
X# First a note about syntax:
X#    The first token in a configuration line represents a mime type.
X#    A mime type is of the form "SubGroup/Item" and either token
X#    can be replaced by the wildcard character "*"
X# 
X#    the second token is the command that you would run
X#    when the specified mime type is encountered.  An example
X#    would be "xv %s" where %s will be replaced by a filename
X#
X#    remaining tokens represent name value pairs.
X#
X#    the "test=<SOME TEST COMMAND>" pair invokes the test command
X#    specified and checks the UNIX shell return code to see if the
X#    test passed.  If the test passes the mime mapping is made, otherwise
X#    the mapping is not made.
X#
X#    the "stream-buffer-size=<NUMBER>" pair tells the application
X#    to use a popen command to invoke the program specified and to
X#    pipe all data into the command.  Only programs that can handle
X#    piped input can use this method.  When this method is used
X#    a "%s" should not be included in the command.  You should
X#    make sure that the program you wish to use can accept piped
X#    data by trying something like this before using it:
X#       cat "YOUR_DATA_FILE_HERE" | COMMAND_TO_TEST
X#
X#    You may continue lines by using the backslash character "\"
X
X# FreeBSD doesn't seem to support `sfplay' yet, but if a port comes
X# along then the following should be enabled.
X#
X# plays AIFF files
X#audio/x-aiff; sfplay %s
X
X# plays AIFC files
X#audio/x-aifc; sfplay %s
X
X# FreeBSD has no wavplay utility either, but if someone
X# would care to port one then this can also be used.
X# plays WAV files
X#audio/x-wav; wavplay %s > /dev/null 2>&1
X
X# FreeBSD /dev/audio is sun compliant
Xaudio/basic; cat %s > /dev/audio
X
X# use this to play MPEG audio if you have maplay installed  
Xaudio/x-mpeg; maplay -; stream-buffer-size=2000
X
X# use this to play MPEG video files if you have mpeg_play installed
Xvideo/mpeg; mpeg_play -quiet %s
X
X# All other anims go well with xanim (if installed)
Xvideo/*; xanim %s > /dev/null 2>&1
X
X# use this to display postscript files
Xapplication/postscript; ghostview %s
X
X# use this to display pdf files
Xapplication/pdf; xpdf %s 2>/dev/null
X
X# this is the default for non GIF or JPEG images
Ximage/*; xv %s
X
X# if you would prefer an external viewer for GIF and JPEG images
X# uncomment the following lines for XV to act as your external viewer.
X#image/gif; xv %s
X#image/jpg; xv %s
X#image/jpeg; xv %s
END-of-linux-netscape45-communicator/files/mailcap
echo x - linux-netscape45-communicator/files/md5
sed 's/^X//' >linux-netscape45-communicator/files/md5 << 'END-of-linux-netscape45-communicator/files/md5'
XMD5 (communicator-v45-export.x86-unknown-linux2.0.tar.gz) = 29ea8e0908062a6dfa1c191ecc0a0081
XMD5 (Fortify-1.3.2-unix-x86.tar.gz) = 2602de68407690cf45c32f9dfef65f74
END-of-linux-netscape45-communicator/files/md5
echo x - linux-netscape45-communicator/files/netscape.sh
sed 's/^X//' >linux-netscape45-communicator/files/netscape.sh << 'END-of-linux-netscape45-communicator/files/netscape.sh'
X#!/bin/sh
Xexport MOZILLA_HOME; MOZILLA_HOME=${MOZILLA_HOME:=@NDIR@}
Xexport CLASSPATH ; CLASSPATH=.:$MOZILLA_HOME
Xexport XCMSDB; XCMSDB=/dev/null
Xexec $MOZILLA_HOME/@NBIN@ $*
END-of-linux-netscape45-communicator/files/netscape.sh
echo c - linux-netscape45-communicator/patches
mkdir -p linux-netscape45-communicator/patches > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - linux-netscape45-communicator/patches/patch-aa
sed 's/^X//' >linux-netscape45-communicator/patches/patch-aa << 'END-of-linux-netscape45-communicator/patches/patch-aa'
X--- ns-install.orig	Tue Oct 13 21:10:13 1998
X+++ ns-install	Sat Nov  7 19:06:15 1998
X@@ -120,6 +120,7 @@
X # Even though the user has already used gzip to extract the file (or
X # we wouldn't be here), make sure we can find it.
X #
X+unset GZIP
X GZIP="gzip"
X flag=1
X while test ${flag} -eq 1
X@@ -294,10 +295,10 @@
X 	# avoid users having to set MOZILLA_JAVA_FONT_PROPERTY_PATH, or
X 	# just assuming Java font-handling is broken.
X 	#
X-	if test ! -z "`ls /bin/uname`"
X+	if test ! -z "`ls /usr/bin/uname`"
X 	then
X 		MOVE_FONTS="TRUE"
X-		case `/bin/uname` in
X+		case `/usr/bin/uname` in
X 			AIX)
X 				PLATFORM="aix"
X 				;;
X@@ -324,14 +325,14 @@
X 	# Is this a virgin install?  If so, create the .netscape dir
X 	# and put the default bookmark file in it.
X 	#
X-	if test ! -d ${HOME}/.netscape
X-	then
X-		# New install, or upgrade from 0.x or 1.x.
X-		mkdir ${HOME}/.netscape
X-		cp ${TARGET}/bookmark.htm ${HOME}/.netscape/bookmarks.html
X-		chmod 644 ${HOME}/.netscape/bookmarks.html
X-		echo "${HOME}/.netscape/bookmarks.html" >> ${LOGFILE}
X-	fi
X+#	if test ! -d ${HOME}/.netscape
X+#	then
X+#		# New install, or upgrade from 0.x or 1.x.
X+#		mkdir ${HOME}/.netscape
X+#		cp ${TARGET}/bookmark.htm ${HOME}/.netscape/bookmarks.html
X+#		chmod 644 ${HOME}/.netscape/bookmarks.html
X+#		echo "${HOME}/.netscape/bookmarks.html" >> ${LOGFILE}
X+#	fi
X fi
X 
X echo ""
END-of-linux-netscape45-communicator/patches/patch-aa
echo x - linux-netscape45-communicator/Makefile
sed 's/^X//' >linux-netscape45-communicator/Makefile << 'END-of-linux-netscape45-communicator/Makefile'
X# New ports collection makefile for:	linux-netscape-communicator-4.5
X# Version required:     4.5
X# Date created:         19 Jan 1998
X# Whom:                 jcwells@u.washignton.edu
X#
X# $Id:$
X#
X
XDISTNAME=	communicator-v45-export.x86-unknown-linux2.0
XPKGNAME=	linux-netscape-communicator-4.5
XCATEGORIES=	www
XMASTER_SITES=	ftp://ftp.netscape.com/pub/communicator/4.5/english/unix/supported/linux20_libc5/complete_install/
X# DISTFILES must be set to begin with so USE_128BIT can add on to it.
X#DISTFILES=	${DISTNAME}${EXTRACT_SUFX}
X
XMAINTAINER=	ports@freebsd.org
X
XRUN_DEPENDS=    /compat/linux/lib/ld.so:${PORTSDIR}/emulators/linux_lib
XBUILD_DEPENDS=  /compat/linux/lib/ld.so:${PORTSDIR}/emulators/linux_lib
X
XPREFIX=		/usr/local/lib
XWRKSRC=         ${WRKDIR}/communicator-v45.x86-unknown-linux2.0
X
XONLY_FOR_ARCHS=	i386
X
XNO_LATEST_LINK=	yes
XNO_BUILD=	Yes
XPLIST_SUB=      NDIRBASE=${NDIRBASE}
XNO_FILTER_SHLIBS=yes
X
XNDIRBASE=	linux-communicator-4.5
XNDIR=		${PREFIX}/${NDIRBASE}
XBINDIR=		${PREFIX}/bin
XNBIN=		communicator-4.5.bin
X
X.if defined(USE_128BIT)
XMASTER_SITES+=	ftp://ftp.fortify.net/pub/Fortify/ \
X		ftp://ftp.za.freebsd.org/pub/Fortify/
XDISTFILES+=	Fortify-1.3.2-unix-x86.tar.gz
XRESTRICTED=	"Contains strong cryptography"
X.else
Xpre-extract:
X		@${ECHO} "You can make Netscape use 128-bit encryption by defining USE_128BIT"
X.endif	# USE_128BIT
X
Xpost-configure:
X	${SED}	-e "s;@NDIR@;${NDIR};g" \
X		-e "s;@NBIN@;${NBIN};g" \
X		${FILESDIR}/netscape.sh >${WRKSRC}/netscape
X
Xdo-install:
X	cd ${WRKSRC} && \
X		yes "" | LC_TIME=C MOZILLA_HOME="${NDIR}" ./ns-install
X	${MV} -f ${NDIR}/netscape ${NDIR}/communicator-4.5.bin
X	${INSTALL_SCRIPT} ${WRKSRC}/netscape ${BINDIR}/communicator-4.5
X	${LN} -sf communicator-4.5 ${BINDIR}/communicator-4
X	${LN} -sf communicator-4 ${BINDIR}/netscape
X	if [ ! -f ${NDIR}/mailcap ] ; then \
X		${INSTALL_DATA} ${FILESDIR}/mailcap ${NDIR}; \
X	fi
X	${RM} -rf ${NDIR}/movemail-src
X.if defined(USE_128BIT)
X	cd ${WRKDIR}/Fortify-1.3.2-unix-x86 && \
X		(${ECHO} yes ; ${ECHO} no ; ${ECHO} no) | ./Fortify.sh ${NDIR}/communicator-4.5.bin
X.endif
X	chown -R ${BINOWN}.${BINGRP} ${NDIR}
